recipes_to_upsert.name: Ninja Chicken Shawarma Rice Bowl

ingredients.item: boneless skinless chicken thighs or chicken breast

instructions: Cut chicken into 2-3 cm pieces. Do not cut too small or it will dry out.
Mix 150 g yogurt, olive oil, lemon juice, grated garlic, paprika, cumin, coriander, turmeric, cinnamon, salt, and black pepper into a thick marinade.
Coat chicken fully in the marinade. Marinate at least 30 minutes; 2-4 hours is better.
Preheat Ninja air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes.
Place chicken in one layer on the crisper plate. Do not crowd; cook in batches if needed.
Air Fry at 200°C for 15 minutes. Shake or turn at minute 8.
Cook another 2-5 minutes if more browning is needed. Chicken should reach 74°C internally.
Rest chicken for 5 minutes before portioning.
Make sauce: mix 250 g yogurt, garlic, lemon, salt, and optional herbs. Add water 1 tbsp at a time until sauce consistency is right.
Serve with 250-320 g cooked rice per portion and Greek salad if desired.
storage: Store chicken, rice, sauce, and salad components separately.
Freeze or vacuum-freeze Sunday portion after cooling.
reheating: Reheat chicken in Ninja at 170°C for 4-6 minutes or microwave 1-2 minutes.
Reheat rice with 1-2 tbsp water under cover.
substitutions: Chicken breast can replace thighs, but it is less juicy.
Potato wedges can replace rice.
Frozen vegetables can replace Greek salad if salad is not desired.
meal_prep_notes: Best as separate components rather than pre-mixed bowls.
Sauce should stay separate until serving.
recipes_to_upsert.why_it_fits_weekly_plan: High-protein structured meal; works for lunch/dinner; supports batch prep and low-friction tracking.

recipes_to_upsert.name: Instant Pro Turkey Potato Chili
recipes_to_upsert.purpose: Dense high-protein batch dish for lunch/dinner.
instructions: Cut potatoes into large 2.5-3 cm cubes so they do not turn into mash under pressure.
Set Instant Pro to Sauté High. Add olive oil, onion, and peppers. Cook 4-5 minutes.
Add garlic, cumin, smoked paprika, paprika, oregano, chili, salt, and pepper. Stir for 30-45 seconds.
Add ground turkey. Break it up with a spatula and cook 5-7 minutes until mostly pale.
Pour in about 100 ml water/broth and scrape the bottom thoroughly to avoid Burn warning.
Add potatoes, beans, chopped tomatoes, tomato paste, and remaining water/broth. Stir.
Pressure Cook High for 6 minutes.
Natural Release for 7 minutes, then release remaining pressure.
Check potatoes. If too firm, simmer on Sauté Medium 3-5 minutes. If soft, do not overcook.
Finish with lemon/lime juice and adjust salt.
Serve 450-550 g chili with 30-60 g Greek yogurt/skyr on top.
storage: Vacuum-freeze 1-2 portions for Sunday or later.
reheating: Or reheat in Instant Pro on Sauté Medium 5-7 minutes with 1-2 tbsp water.
substitutions: Potato can be reduced if serving with rice.
Beans can be replaced with chickpeas.
Ground chicken can replace ground turkey.
meal_prep_notes: This is a main batch dish. Portion into 450-550 g containers.
Use yogurt topping only at serving.
recipes_to_upsert.why_it_fits_weekly_plan: High-protein, high-satiety, batch-friendly meal that replaces sweet potato with regular potato due to availability.

recipes_to_upsert.name: Ninja Greek Turkey Feta Meatballs
recipes_to_upsert.purpose: Reusable protein base for lunch, dinner, and breakfast shakshuka.

ingredients.item: cucumber, grated and squeezed for sauce

instructions: Mix ground turkey, egg, panko, feta, onion, garlic, herbs, oregano, paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper.
Mix only until combined. Do not overwork the meat.
If mixture is too wet, add 10-15 g extra panko. If too dry, add 1-2 tbsp yogurt.
Form 24-28 meatballs, 35-45 g each.
Preheat Ninja to 200°C for 3 minutes.
Place meatballs in one layer. Air Fry at 200°C for 8 minutes.
Turn or shake, then cook 3-5 minutes more until 74°C internally.
Rest 5 minutes.
For sauce, mix yogurt, lemon, garlic, grated squeezed cucumber, salt, and optional dill.
Serve 4-5 meatballs with potato wedges and sauce.
substitutions: Chicken mince can replace turkey.
Oat flour can replace panko.
Mozzarella can replace feta, but the Greek flavor is reduced.
meal_prep_notes: Use as lunch/dinner protein or breakfast protein in shakshuka.
recipes_to_upsert.why_it_fits_weekly_plan: Gives a more interesting high-protein base than plain turkey and works across several meals.

recipes_to_upsert.name: Ninja Sesame Tuna Steak

ingredients.item: lemon or lime juice for sauce

instructions: If frozen, thaw tuna in the fridge or sealed in cold water.
Pat tuna dry very well.
Mix soy sauce, lemon/lime, oil, and black pepper.
Coat tuna and marinate 10 minutes only.
Press sesame seeds onto both sides.
Preheat Ninja to 200°C for 5 minutes.
Cook 2-3 minutes, turn, then 2-3 minutes more depending on thickness and desired doneness.
For fully cooked fish, target 63°C internally. Less cooked is a personal risk tradeoff.
Rest 1-2 minutes and slice.
Mix yogurt, mustard, soy sauce, and lemon/lime for sauce.
storage: If leftovers remain, refrigerate and use within 1-2 days.
reheating: If reheating, Ninja 180-200°C for 1-2 minutes only.
substitutions: Two cans of tuna can replace the steak using the tuna egg potato salad recipe.
Potato or rice can be used as the carb.
meal_prep_notes: Do not batch-cook all tuna steaks for the whole week.
recipes_to_upsert.why_it_fits_weekly_plan: Adds a fresher, more interesting high-protein dinner without complex cooking.

recipes_to_upsert.name: Tuna Egg Potato Salad
recipes_to_upsert.purpose: Protein salad fallback or planned lunch.
instructions: If eggs are not prepared, boil eggs 9-10 minutes, then chill and peel.
Cut cooked potato into 2 cm pieces.
Mix yogurt, mustard, lemon juice, black pepper, and a small amount of salt.
In a bowl combine drained tuna, chopped eggs, potato, pickles, and herbs.
Add sauce and fold gently so the potatoes do not turn into mash.
Taste. Usually add more lemon/pepper before adding more salt.
Serve as a bowl or with 1-2 toasts if more energy is needed.
storage: Already mixed salad should be eaten within 24-36 hours.
Store components separately for better quality.
substitutions: Chicken shawarma can replace tuna.
Rice can replace potato.
A small amount of light mayo can be mixed with yogurt if desired.
meal_prep_notes: Keep tuna in cans, eggs boiled, potatoes separate, and sauce separate until serving.
recipes_to_upsert.why_it_fits_weekly_plan: Fast, high-protein, non-sweet fallback that uses pantry tuna and prepared potatoes.

recipes_to_upsert.name: Tuna Feta Breakfast Omelet Roll
recipes_to_upsert.purpose: Fast savory breakfast after shopping or on low-prep mornings.
instructions: Drain tuna well and crumble feta.
Beat eggs with black pepper and oregano.
Heat pan on medium and add olive oil.
Add pepper for 1-2 minutes if using.
Pour in eggs. After 30-40 seconds add tuna and feta.
Cook 3-5 minutes, folding into an omelet or scrambling gently.
Serve in flatbread or with toast.
If protein still feels low, add 20-30 g whey with water after the meal.
substitutions: Cooked chicken shawarma can replace tuna.
Cheese can replace feta.
meal_prep_notes: Keep tuna cans, feta, and chopped pepper ready; add eggs fresh.
recipes_to_upsert.why_it_fits_weekly_plan: Savory, high-satiety breakfast that avoids oatmeal and bought sweets.

recipes_to_upsert.name: Shawarma Breakfast Omelet
recipes_to_upsert.purpose: Savory high-protein breakfast using chicken shawarma leftovers.
instructions: Heat chicken shawarma in a pan for 1-2 minutes.
Beat eggs with black pepper.
Pour eggs over the chicken.
Add feta or cheese.
Cook as a scramble or folded omelet for 3-4 minutes.
Serve with flatbread/toast.
Add yogurt garlic sauce on the plate, not in the hot pan.
reheating: Not recommended as a finished omelet.
substitutions: Turkey meatballs, chopped, can replace chicken.
Tuna can replace chicken.
meal_prep_notes: Prepare breakfast kits with 150-180 g cooked chicken and cheese; add eggs fresh.
recipes_to_upsert.why_it_fits_weekly_plan: Uses batch protein in a satisfying non-sweet breakfast.

recipes_to_upsert.name: Meatball Shakshuka
recipes_to_upsert.purpose: Savory breakfast using Greek turkey meatballs.
instructions: Add chopped tomatoes/passata to a pan with paprika, oregano, and black pepper.
Simmer on medium heat for 3-4 minutes.
Add meatballs and warm through for 2-3 minutes.
Make 3 small wells and crack in the eggs.
Cover with lid and cook 4-6 minutes until whites are set.
Top with feta.
Serve with toast or flatbread if extra carbs are needed.
reheating: Not recommended after eggs are cooked.
substitutions: Chicken shawarma can replace meatballs.
More tomatoes can be used if a saucier breakfast is preferred.
meal_prep_notes: Keep meatballs cooked and tomato sauce available; add eggs fresh.
recipes_to_upsert.why_it_fits_weekly_plan: High-satiety breakfast aligned with the user's preference for omelets/meaty breakfasts.

recipes_to_upsert.name: Chicken Potato Frittata

ingredients.item: cooked chicken shawarma or cooked chicken

instructions: Cut potato wedges into smaller pieces if large.
Heat pan on medium and add oil.
Add potato and chicken. Warm 2-3 minutes.
Beat eggs with pepper and oregano.
Pour eggs over chicken and potato.
Add feta/cheese.
Cover and cook on low-medium heat 6-8 minutes until set.
If top is still loose, finish under grill/broiler if available or cook covered 2 minutes more.
reheating: Can be reheated once, but fresh is better.
substitutions: Turkey meatballs can replace chicken.
Toast can replace some potato.
meal_prep_notes: Use this to close out leftover chicken and potato on Sunday.
recipes_to_upsert.why_it_fits_weekly_plan: Uses leftovers efficiently while preserving a savory, satiating breakfast.

recipes_to_upsert.name: Chicken Shawarma Loaded Flatbread / Bowl
recipes_to_upsert.purpose: More interesting dinner format using chicken shawarma components.
instructions: Reheat chicken shawarma in Ninja at 170°C for 4-6 minutes or in a pan.
Warm flatbread briefly if using.
Add chicken, Greek salad components, feta, and yogurt garlic sauce.
If making a bowl, use cooked rice instead of flatbread.
Finish with lemon juice.
reheating: Reheat only chicken/rice/flatbread. Do not heat fresh salad or sauce.
substitutions: Meatballs can replace chicken.
Rice can replace flatbread.
meal_prep_notes: This is a fast assembly meal from prepared components.
recipes_to_upsert.why_it_fits_weekly_plan: Keeps the menu interesting while using the same batch-prepped protein base.
